title: MirrorTrace Data-Broker Opt-Out Input
description: Builds a self-removal workflow for the SELF subject's OWN data-broker listings. A broker opt-out is a first-person erasure ('remove the record about ME'), so the scope_type enum DELIBERATELY omits public_figure / brand / safety_evidence — you cannot opt anyone else out of a broker. Only self (and consented, which requires written authorization) are accepted. NO scraping happens: you supply listings you have personally CONFIRMED are about you.

scope_type.title: Scope type
scope_type.description: A data-broker opt-out removes the record about the requester, so it is only ever 'self'. 'consented' is permitted ONLY with a written authorization URL (a real person who agreed in writing). public_figure / brand / safety_evidence are intentionally NOT offered — they cannot file a broker self-removal.
enumTitles: Consented (someone who authorized me, in writing)

subject_label.title: Your name (for the erasure letter)
subject_label.description: The name to put on the GDPR/CCPA erasure request. If omitted, the letter shows an explicit [[ FILL IN ]] placeholder — never a fabricated name.

authorization_evidence_url.title: Authorization evidence URL (consented only)
authorization_evidence_url.description: Required when scope_type=consented: a URL to written consent from the subject. Without it, the run is refused by the scope gate.

confirmed_listings.title: Broker listings you CONFIRMED are about you
confirmed_listings.description: One object per listing you personally verified is about YOU, e.g. { "broker_id": "spokeo", "listing_url": "https://www.spokeo.com/Your-Name/123", "confirmed_self": true }. broker_id must be a known broker (see README registry). A listing WITHOUT confirmed_self:true is skipped — this actor never scrapes or invents a listing. Private-social URLs are blocked by the scope gate.
